title: Phase B: Remove dropped clients (Windsurf/Roo Code/Gemini CLI)
description: Independent commit 'v2: remove dropped clients (Windsurf/Roo Code/Gemini CLI)' enforcing strict client-kind union. (1) `packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts:5` — drop `.passthrough()`, strict union `claudeCode | cursor | codex`, **unknown keys reject and exit**. (2) `packages/cli/__tests__/fixtures/clientScope.test.ts:8` — `ClientKind` union remove `'Windsurf'` etc. (3) 6 files containing `windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI`: `doctor.ts`, `doctor.test.ts`, `schemas-roundtrip.test.ts`, `fabric-config.ts`, `clientScope.test.ts`, `doctor-fix.test.ts` — all references removed (no skip-test, hard delete).

files.change: Drop .passthrough() from the client-config object schema; tighten ClientKind union to strict 'claudeCode' | 'cursor' | 'codex' (no other variants allowed); ensure Zod parse errors on unknown keys cause CLI exit (loud failure, not silent)
Remove 'Windsurf', 'RooCode', 'GeminiCLI' (and any case variants) from ClientKind test fixture union; assert parser rejects them
Remove all branches handling windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI clients; remove client-specific path checks for those three
Delete test cases targeting windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI scenarios; ADD a new test asserting fabric-config rejects unknown client keys with clear error message
Remove fixtures containing windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI; add a negative-path fixture asserting parse failure on unknown client key
Remove all doctor --fix scenarios targeting windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI

implementation: Run `grep -rln 'windsurf\|rooCode\|geminiCLI\|Windsurf\|RooCode\|GeminiCLI' packages/` to confirm exactly 6 files (matches Q2)

In packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts: locate the client-config Zod schema (~line 5); remove `.passthrough()` call; tighten enum/union to literal 'claudeCode' | 'cursor' | 'codex'; export resulting strict schema
In each of the 6 files, delete every reference to windsurf|rooCode|geminiCLI (case-insensitive) — code branches AND comments AND test fixtures
Add a positive test in doctor.test.ts asserting fabric-config parse exits with clear error on unknown client key (e.g. `{ windsurf: { ... } }` → throws ZodError with 'unknown key' in message)
Run `pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint` — must all exit 0
Re-run grep gate — must be empty

reference.pattern: Strict-union client-kind enforcement; fail-loud on unknown
reference.examples: Zod strict() / no-passthrough — see Zod docs; reject + exit pattern (not warn-and-continue)

criteria: No matches for `windsurf` (case-insensitive) in `packages/` (grep returns empty)
No matches for `rooCode\|RooCode` in `packages/` (grep returns empty)
No matches for `geminiCLI\|GeminiCLI` in `packages/` (grep returns empty)
fabric-config.ts schema does NOT contain `.passthrough()` call
fabric-config.ts ClientKind union has exactly 3 literals: claudeCode, cursor, codex
New negative-path test exists asserting parse failure on unknown client key

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: schema event-type rename (TASK-006), version/release artifacts (TASK-008), dogfood (TASK-007). Strictly scoped to client-kind enforcement.

rationale.chosen_approach: Strict-union enforcement via Zod (drop .passthrough), fail-loud on unknown client keys
alternatives_considered: Keep .passthrough() and just remove explicit handlers
decision_factors: Memory: Fabric supports only Claude Code / Cursor / Codex CLI
Loud failure prevents silent mis-config in customer projects
Clean-slate preference (zero users, no migration tax)
rationale.tradeoffs: Customers who somehow had a windsurf entry get a hard error on next `fab` run instead of a warning — acceptable per the zero-user clean-slate decision

manual_checks: Construct a test fabric-config with `windsurf: {}` key — confirm `node packages/cli/dist/index.js doctor` exits non-zero with ZodError mentioning unknown key
Construct a valid 3-client config — confirm `fab doctor` exit 0
success_metrics: Negative test for unknown client key passes
integration: doctor command on dropped-client config exits with clear error
